results.title: Impeller | KSB
results.snippet: Impeller An impeller is a rotating component equipped with vanes or blades used in turbomachinery (e.g. centrifugal pumps). Flow deflection at the impeller vanes allows mechanical power (energy at the vanes) to be converted into pump power output.

results.title: Impellers Selection Guide: Types, Features, Applications - GlobalSpec
results.snippet: Impellers (also spelled impellors or impellars) are rotating devices designed to alter the flow and/or pressure of liquids, gases, and vapors. Impellers consist of various vanes — often blade-shaped — arranged around a short central shaft. When the shaft and vanes rotate, they suck in fluids or gase

results.title: PDF Impeller Blade Design Method for Centrifugal Compressors
results.snippet: The design of a centrifugal impeller must yield blades that are aero- dynamically efficient, easy to manufacture, and mechanically sound. The blade design method described here satisfies the first two criteria and with a judicious choice of certain variables will also satisfy stress considerations.

results.title: Impeller Design: Types, Applications, and Simulation - SimScale
results.snippet: Blade Shape and Curvature: Curved blades guide fluid better than straight ones, reducing turbulence and energy losses. The blade shape is tailored to specific applications, such as radial, mixed-flow, or axial-flow impellers. Impeller Width: Impeller width affects flow rate and efficiency.

results.title: Centrifugal Impellers: Structure, Function, and Applications
results.snippet: A centrifugal impeller is a rotating component, typically disk-shaped, with curved blades or vanes that accelerate fluid radially outward.
